instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `ensure-list` that evaluates an expression once; if the value is a list, return it, otherwise signal an error showing the offending value.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (GET-ITEMS)))
            (UNLESS (LISTP #:G1) (ERROR "Expected list, got ~S" #:G1))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (GET-ITEMS)))
            (UNLESS (LISTP #:G112) (ERROR "Expected list, got ~S" #:G112))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
